title: Spacing & Tokens
intro: The 8-based spacing scale, four-channel status colors, and layout-layer neutrals that replace the hundred-plus arbitrary values scattered across legacy screens. If you are typing a raw hex code or a stray pixel value, the right token already exists — use it.

blocks.title: The Laws of Spacing & Tokens
blocks.intro: These rules govern every hi-fi screen. The tokens live in `demo/src/tokens.css` and are available globally — no import needed in CSS modules.

items.rule: Every spacing value maps to a token on the 8-based scale: 0, 4, 8, 12, 16, 24, 32, 40, 48.
items.why: Values like 18px or 20px sit between steps — each one forks the rhythm and can never be retuned centrally.

items.rule: Parent padding is always at least one step above child gaps.
items.why: Equal or inverted spacing collapses hierarchy; content reads as crowded siblings instead of contained children.
The 4x contrast binds a label to its input — loosen the pair and the form dissolves into floating text.

items.rule: Spacing on layout primitives goes through props — `<Horizontal gap={12}>`, `<Vertical flex='1'>` — never style objects.
items.why: Style-object spacing bypasses the primitives' layout contract and fails review per the repo mistakes table.

items.rule: Status color comes from the four-channel tokens `--status-{tone}-{solid|text|bg|border}`, never raw hex.
items.why: Raw hex copies are tiny forks of the palette — a designer retune reaches tokens, not the 113 files holding pasted values.

items.rule: Channels travel as a set: bg + border + text compose pills; solid drives dots and filled accents. Never mix channels across tones.
items.why: Each tone's channels are contrast-tested together — a success bg under warning text has no guaranteed legibility.

items.rule: Neutral greys come from `--border-subtle`/`--border-default` and `--surface-muted`/`--surface-sunken`, not hex and not Excalibrr `--gray-*`.
items.why: `--gray-*` belongs to the theme layer and inverts across themes (`--gray-100` is near-white). The layout neutrals are theme-stable.

items.rule: When no token fits, take the nearest step — never invent a value.
items.why: A near-miss token keeps the page on the shared rhythm; a custom pixel value starts a second scale.

blocks.title: The Scale
blocks.caption: Nine steps, 8-based with 4px and 12px half-steps. Every spacing value in the app resolves to one of these tokens.

blocks.title: Token Widths at 1:1
blocks.caption: Relative size of each step at actual pixel width. The jump from space-4 to space-5 (16px to 24px) is where 'inside a card' becomes 'between cards'.

blocks.title: Nesting Hierarchy
blocks.caption: Each container's padding sits at least one step above its children's gaps — 48px page padding stepping down to the 4px label-input gap. The right column shows the anti-pattern: gaps equal to or larger than their parent's padding.

blocks.title: Token-by-Situation Map
blocks.caption: Layout, cards, forms, and inline clusters each claim a fixed slice of the scale — no situation requires an off-scale value.

blocks.title: Status Color Tokens
blocks.caption: Five tones, four channels each. Background + border + text compose a readable pill at any size; solid drives dots and filled accents. StatusBadge consumes all four channels automatically.

blocks.title: Neutral Surfaces & Borders
blocks.caption: Layout-layer neutrals: two border weights and two muted surfaces replace hand-typed #e8e8e8 and #fafafa greys.

blocks.title: Token Discipline, Side by Side
blocks.caption: Scale-based spacing and channel tokens on the left; arbitrary pixels and forked hex on the right. The don't column's field gap exceeds its card padding — the container stops containing.

blocks.title: Spacing Scale
blocks.intro: Defined in `tokens.css` as `--space-0` through `--space-8`. In CSS use `var(--space-N)`; in JSX `gap`/`padding` props use the raw number.

rows.use: Compact: button group gaps, inline pairs
Snug: card inner padding, footer bars
Default: form field gaps, section inner padding
Comfortable: section gaps, card padding, page content
Spacious: major section breaks, page dividers

blocks.title: Neutral Surfaces & Borders
blocks.intro: Theme-stable greys for layout chrome. Use these instead of hex and instead of Excalibrr's theme-owned `--gray-*` ramp.

rows.use: Footer bars, striped rows, code blocks

blocks.title: Status Color Channels
blocks.intro: Each tone exposes four channels: `-solid` (dots, filled chips, accents), `-text` (AA on its own `-bg`), `-bg` (tinted background), `-border` (pairs with `-bg`). Values listed solid · text · bg · border.

blocks.title: Canonical CSS Module
blocks.note: Tokens are globally available in any CSS module — no import. The chip pulls bg + border + text as a set; the dot is the only place solid appears.

blocks.title: Canonical JSX Spacing
blocks.note: gap={16} not style={{ gap: '16px' }}; flex='1' not style={{ flex: 1 }}. GraviButton renders buttonText, not children, and takes theme booleans (theme1, success) — never type='primary'.

items.do: Step the hierarchy down: card padding space-5 (24px), field gap space-4 (16px), label gap space-1 (4px).
items.dont: 10px card padding around a 20px field gap — the gap exceeds its container.
items.why: Containment is communicated by padding outweighing internal gaps; invert that and the card stops reading as a container.

items.do: gap: var(--space-4); padding: var(--space-5) — pull spacing from tokens.css.
items.dont: gap: 20px; padding: 18px — values that exist on no scale.
items.why: Token values retune in one file; stray pixels can't be found, let alone changed.

items.do: background: var(--status-success-bg) with its matching border and text channels.
items.why: Identical pixels today, but the hex copy is invisible to every future palette change.

blocks.title: Where Each Token Lives
blocks.body:
Layout: page wrapper padding `space-8` (48px); between major sections `space-6` (32px); page content area `space-5` (24px).

Cards & panels: card body padding `space-5` (24px); card header padding `space-4` (16px); footer bar padding `space-3` (12px).

Forms: between form sections `space-5` (24px); between fields `space-4` (16px); label to input `space-1` (4px).

Buttons & inline: button group gap `space-2` (8px); icon to text `space-1` (4px); inline element pairs `space-2` (8px).

items.title: --gray-100 is near-white, not near-black
items.detail: Excalibrr's theme greys run opposite to the usual convention and swap across themes. For layout chrome, use the theme-stable neutrals: --border-subtle, --border-default, --surface-muted, --surface-sunken.

items.title: --theme-color-2 changes hue across themes
items.detail: It swings between green and blue depending on the active theme. Pin brand accents to --theme-color-1.

items.title: Texto appearance='secondary' is blue, not gray
items.detail: For muted gray text use appearance='medium'. 'secondary' maps to the secondary brand color and will read as a link.

items.title: Size-tier tokens exist for overlays too
items.detail: tokens.css also defines --modal-sm/md/lg/xl (480-1080px), --drawer-right-narrow/standard/wide, and --drawer-bottom-compact/standard/full. Pull a tier instead of typing a width; the Modals and Drawers entries prescribe which tier fits which job.
